Steps.Message:
Sent by Luke at 22:51 - Our engineers have investigated and found that the power is off due to a fault on our underground electricity network which has affected our substation equipment, so we' have a specialist team on site to pinpoint the fault and dig down to expose the cabling for repairs.

Considering the work that may be required, the revised estimated time to have the power back on is between 01:30 and 02:30.

We're sorry to change the time, but we'll keep you updated with our progress. If you have any questions or concerns, our team are always here to talk on 105.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Bev at 21:35 - Thanks for bearing with us tonight.

Our engineers are checking our substation equipment to ensure our network is healthy before they attempt to restore your power. They may also walk our cabling route throughout the area to check for any visible problems, check smaller electrical sites in the area, or apply specialist equipment which may restore power temporarily to help them with their testing.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Talie at 07:00 - Our engineers have restored power to all 3461 properties in the Hullbridge SS5 6 area, and we're pleased that this is sooner than we estimated. They have diverted power around the underground cable fault.

Every outage is fully investigated by our engineers, though power may be lost several times before we can safely find and fix the issue. Steps.Message:
Sent by David at 07:57 - Thanks for your patience this morning.

Our staff are checking our equipment throughout the area to pinpoint the issue on our underground cabling network. In addition to this, they are liaising closely with our control room to find safe and reliable ways to divert power around any problems and get everyone restored.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them. We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:

Steps.Message:
Sent by Talie at 07:12- Engineers are on their way to investigate the cause of the voltage issues in the Wisbech, PE14 8 area.

We appreciate that power flickering and lights dimming can be concerning. Usually, this is due to interference with cabling and can sometimes cause network safety equipment to partially operate, meaning not all power is coming through to your property as normal.

At the moment our time frame to have power back on is between 08:30 and 09:30, but once our staff have fully assessed the situation this could change. Also in some cases we have to restore power in stages; so it's normal to continue receiving messages for a while even after your power has come back on.

We'll be in touch again when we arrive on site.

Steps.Message:
Sent by David at 12:37 - Hi David here again. Thanks for your patience over today.

Our engineers have let us know that our engineers have run into complications with the ongoing work to restore your power due to the faulty overhead electrical equipment, this is hindering our progress to restore your power. To give them time to do this safely, the estimated time frame to restore your power is now between 14:30-15:30.

We're sorry this is taking longer than expected, and we'll continue to keep you up to date, but if any change to this time comes in from our staff I'll let you know as soon as we hear.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by David at 11:45 - Thanks for your patience this morning.

Our staff are checking our equipment throughout the area to pinpoint the issue on our overhead cabling network. In addition to this, they are liaising closely with our control room to find safe and reliable ways to divert power around any problems and get everyone restored.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them. We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

For more information just click here: https://UKPN.short.gy/PSR If you have any further concerns or questions though, don't hesitate to call us free on 08003163105. We'll continue to keep you updated with our engineer's progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Our engineers have arrived on site in the DA13 0 area and are investigating the fault. However, we have managed to divert power back to the majority of properties, so Specialist engineers from our control room will continue to divert power remotely, so your power may come back on at any point. Sometimes we can't restore everyone this way, so we're also sending staff to check our equipment. We're currently estimating power will be on between 15:30-16:30, but this may change depending on what our staff find. Power back on? To stop receiving texts, follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe. Otherwise, we'll keep you updated.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage affects multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you expect. We're arranging staff to attend ASAP, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting electricity. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to get as many customers back on as we can.

Once available, our engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, they will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will work to locate the issue and make repairs, which may take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing but we'll always give you the clearest information we have.
